purpose: correct the contaminated paired no-actor render in CF-I-OBS-001 and repeat all frozen indicator decisions unchanged

correction.diagnosis: Camera.__init__ uses a shared mutable default dynamics dictionary and gaussian_renderer.render writes planned actors into it in place, so the actor persisted into the paired no-actor render
correction.only_permitted_change: clear the shared Camera default dynamics dictionary immediately before both members of every paired render
correction.unchanged: states, frames, cameras, thresholds, projections, controls, and claim boundary

controls.empty_actor_pair: render each selected state again with the dynamic actor map removed while camera pose and scene remain fixed

indicators.CF-I-O1: planner state to render transform fidelity with a time-shifted state negative
indicators.CF-I-O2: independent camera projection versus paired RGB actor-support membership with a rotated-camera negative
indicators.CF-I-O3: paired RGB actor support inside a dilated projected box with a shifted-projection negative
indicators.CF-I-O4: rendered observation divergence does not precede the declared state cause, with an early timestamp-label negative

stop_rule: if any indicator fails to discriminate its frozen positive and negative controls, stop before adding an AD receiver and diagnose the observation transport gap
independence_notice: Projection and RGB pairing are recomputed outside the HUGSIM scorer and do not use HUGSIM semantic, depth, NC, TTC, PDMS, or a detector. State, calibration, vehicle dimensions, RGB, and scene nevertheless remain HUGSIM-produced and are not independent real-world truth.
strongest_allowed_claim: Within the selected scene-0383 rear-actor window, frozen planner state, camera membership, projected RGB location, and causal onset transport consistently into HUGSIM rendered observations.
forbidden_claims: HUGSIM RGB is equivalent to a real camera
semantic or depth output is correct
the AttackPlanner behavior is realistic traffic behavior
an AD receiver perceives or responds correctly
closed-loop safety or overall simulator credibility is established
